decks.notes: The main strategy of this deck is to quickly deploy low-cost creatures like Delver of Secrets and Tolarian Terror, supported by spells like Counterspell and Dispel to protect them. Card draw and manipulation spells like Brainstorm and Ponder help ensure a steady stream of threats, while Lórien Revealed provides an additional source of card advantage. Cryptic Serpent offers a powerful late-game threat, while Sleep of the Dead can potentially lock down the opponent's board. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and maintain control over the game.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through the use of Gates cards and various removal spells. By utilizing cards like Prismatic Strands and Malevolent Rumble, along with efficient creatures like Seeker of Skybreak and Outlaw Medic, the goal is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while establishing a solid board presence. The inclusion of Manor Gate and Citadel Gate provide additional utility and flexibility in managing resources effectively. Overall, this deck aims to outmaneuver and outlast opponents through strategic use of control elements and Gates synergy.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with creatures using cards like Balustrade Spy and Dread Return, then use Lotleth Giant to finish off the opponent by sacrificing the creatures in the graveyard. The deck also features a lot of mana ramping creatures to help cast the combo pieces quickly. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help to refill the hand with creatures, while Avenging Hunter provides some additional aggression. The deck aims to set up the combo as quickly as possible to win the game.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying cheap artifacts to take advantage of the affinity mechanic, allowing for explosive plays and overwhelming the opponent with a large number of threats. By utilizing cards like Myr Enforcer and Refurbished Familiar, along with efficient removal spells like Galvanic Blast and Terminate, the deck can efficiently control the board while pressuring the opponent's life total. The inclusion of card draw spells like Thoughtcast and Ichor Wellspring ensures the deck can maintain its aggressive game plan and stay ahead in resources.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game through the use of gates and spells while utilizing creatures like Seeker of Skybreak and Outlaw Medic to maintain board presence and gain advantage. The deck aims to slow down the opponent's strategy with removal spells like Journey to Nowhere and Prismatic Strands, while also utilizing cards like Pursue the Past and Bitter Reunion to generate card advantage. The deck also includes combat tricks like Malevolent Rumble and Hawkeye's Bow to deal with threats efficiently.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent through a combination of aggressive creatures and burn spells. Cards like Guttersnipe and Kessig Flamebreather provide consistent damage while spells like Fiery Temper, Lightning Bolt, and Fireblast can quickly finish off the opponent. Highway Robbery and Grab the Prize help to maintain card advantage and Faithless Looting provides card selection. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and relentless assault of damage.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize mana acceleration and cost reduction effects to quickly cast spells that generate mana and create a storm of triggers. By chaining together spells like Seething Song and Glimpse the Impossible, the deck aims to generate an overwhelming amount of red mana and storm count to enable a massive finisher like Big Score. The deck also includes cards like Thornscape Familiar and Wrenn's Resolve to further support the combo and protect key pieces.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly with cards like Thornscape Familiar and Manamorphose to cast spells such as Seething Song and Big Score. It utilizes Goblin Anarchomancer to reduce the cost of its spells and combo off with cards like Glimpse the Impossible and Seize the Storm. The goal is to ramp up mana production and cast multiple spells in a turn to overwhelm the opponent and win the game quickly. The deck also includes cards like First Day of Class and Reckless Impulse for card draw and filtering.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disruption, removal, and card advantage to control the board and outvalue the opponent. It uses counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to interact with the opponent's threats while developing its own game plan. The deck also includes evasive creatures like Ninja of the Deep Hours and Spellstutter Sprite to pressure the opponent and maintain card advantage. The mana base is optimized for consistency and includes utility lands like Bojuka Bog for graveyard hate. Overall, the deck aims to control the game and eventually win through incremental advantage.

This deck focuses on utilizing gate lands to fix mana and gain life, while controlling the board with removal spells and protection effects. It aims to outlast opponents through recurring creatures and life gain, ultimately winning through attrition. The key strategy involves establishing a solid defense with Guardian of the Guildpact and Prismatic Strands, while also gaining incremental advantage with cards like Gatecreeper Vine and Outlaw Medic. By maximizing the value of gate lands and synergistic cards, the deck aims to grind out victories against more aggressive opponents.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of removal spells and recurring threats. By utilizing cards like Cauldron Familiar and Troll of Khazad-dûm, along with sacrifice outlets like Fanatical Offering and Campfire, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent by generating card advantage and recurring creatures. With a mix of creature removal, card draw, and graveyard hate, the deck is able to adapt to different matchups and disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win conditions.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying cheap aggressive creatures while disrupting the opponent's game plan with removal spells and discard effects. The key strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a continuous stream of threats and disruption, utilizing versatile cards like Gixian Infiltrator and Nihil Spellbomb to maintain board control while pressuring the opponent. By combining aggressive creatures with disruptive elements, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and outpace the opponent's strategies, ultimately leading to victory through a combination of damage and card advantage.

The deck focuses on using sacrifice effects to generate value and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By utilizing cards like Writhing Chrysalis and Fanatical Offering to sacrifice creatures, the deck aims to control the board and outvalue the opponent. With a combination of removal spells like Cast Down and disruption tools like Nihil Spellbomb, the deck looks to grind out victories while pressuring the opponent with aggressive creatures like Gixian Infiltrator. The manabase is composed of artifact lands and land destruction spells to further disrupt the opponent's resources.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The strategy revolves around dealing damage quickly and efficiently, utilizing cards like Voldaren Epicure, Kessig Flamebreather, and Lightning Bolt to chip away at the opponent's life total. The deck also focuses on card advantage and reaching critical mass with cards like Faithless Looting and Grab the Prize. By maintaining pressure and applying constant threats, the goal is to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on casting a large number of spells in a single turn to generate mana and draw cards in order to ramp into big spells like Big Score and Seize the Storm. Thornscape Familiar helps reduce the cost of red spells, while Goblin Anarchomancer provides additional mana when casting spells. The goal is to quickly assemble the necessary mana to cast a lethal storm spell or deal massive damage with Burning Curiosity. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells and finish the game in a single explosive turn.

The key strategy of this deck is to generate a large amount of mana using the Urzatron lands and ramp spells, while controlling the opponent's board with removal spells and artifacts. The deck aims to survive the early game with defensive creatures and utility spells, before transitioning into a powerful late game with explosive plays using cards like Energy Refractor and Ashnod's Altar. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with big threats like Fangren Marauder and Golem Foundry, while disrupting their game plan with Nihil Spellbomb and other disruption cards.

This deck focuses on quickly getting key combo pieces onto the battlefield and chaining together powerful spells to win the game in a single explosive turn. By using cards like Balustrade Spy to flip the entire deck into the graveyard and then using Dread Return to bring back a game-ending creature like Lotleth Giant, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its combo potential. The deck also includes mana ramp creatures like Overgrown Battlement and Wall of Roots to quickly build up to its combo pieces.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the Balustrade Spy combo to mill your entire library and then use Dread Return to reanimate Lotleth Giant, dealing lethal damage to your opponent. The deck also includes a variety of creatures and spells to help ramp up your mana, dig for combo pieces, and protect your key creatures. The goal is to overwhelm your opponent with a powerful combo finish while controlling the board and gaining card advantage through the use of Lead the Stampede and Winding Way.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to efficiently control the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage. By disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells like Counterspell and Spellstutter Sprite, removing threats with cards like Snuff Out and Agony Warp, and drawing cards with Brainstorm and Lórien Revealed, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent while keeping the board under control. This deck also utilizes creatures like Ninja of the Deep Hours and Thorn of the Black Rose to provide additional card advantage and pressure on the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful control elements in the form of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain control of the board. By quickly filling up the graveyard with cards like Thought Scour and Mental Note, the deck can efficiently utilize delve cards like Gurmag Angler and Tolarian Terror. The inclusion of Murmuring Mystic and Sneaky Snacker allows for incremental advantage to be gained over time, while cards like Abandon Attachments and Lórien Revealed provide additional utility and card advantage.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying cheap artifact creatures and utilizing affinity to reduce their cost, while also generating value through sacrificing artifacts. It aims to overwhelm opponents with a swarm of creatures and direct damage spells, while also disrupting their graveyard strategies with Nihil Spellbomb. The deck's key strategy is to efficiently generate card advantage and deal damage by sacrificing artifacts and utilizing recursion effects. It combines aggressive beatdown tactics with resource denial and card advantage to outpace opponents and secure victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing powerful creatures with haste and high damage output to pressure opponents early on. By ramping up mana with Arbor Elf, Utopia Sprawl, and Wild Growth, the deck can quickly deploy threats like Avenging Hunter and Fang Dragon. Boarding Party and Silumgar Scavenger provide additional card advantage, while Writhing Chrysalis and Malevolent Rumble offer removal options. Lead the Stampede helps refill the hand with creatures, ensuring a continuous stream of threats to wear down the opponent and secure a victory.

This burn aggro deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent through a combination of efficient creatures and direct damage spells. By consistently attacking with creatures like Kessig Flamebreather and Voldaren Epicure, while also using spells like Fireblast and Lightning Bolt to hit the opponent directly, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero. The inclusion of cards like Grab the Prize and Faithless Looting helps with card advantage and allows the deck to maintain pressure on the opponent throughout the game.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize cards that enable you to control the game by countering opponent spells, milling their deck, and generating power through cards like Tolarian Terror and Cryptic Serpent. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while simultaneously working to build up your own board presence. By efficiently managing your resources and leveraging cards like Counterspell and Brainstorm, you can assert your dominance in the game and outmaneuver your opponent to secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly cycle through your deck with cards like Street Wraith and Architects of Will, generating mana with cards like Dark Ritual and Cabal Ritual to cast Songs of the Damned, and then utilize Reaping the Graves to bring back important combo pieces like Drannith Stinger and Drannith Healer. The goal is to create a loop where you can continuously cast spells and generate enough mana to win the game in a single explosive turn.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly generate mana through rituals and other spells, in order to cast powerful spells like Big Score and Seething Song. Thornscape Familiar and Goblin Anarchomancer help reduce the cost of spells, while Glimpse the Impossible helps dig for combo pieces. Pirate's Pillage and Reckless Impulse provide card advantage, while Wrenn's Resolve protects against removal. The goal is to set up a big turn where multiple spells can be cast in succession, leading to a victory through overwhelming the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly generate mana through cards like Seething Song and Manamorphose while using cards like Goblin Anarchomancer and Thornscape Familiar to reduce the cost of spells. The deck aims to combo off using cards like Big Score and Laughing Mad to deal massive amounts of damage to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Seize the Storm and Glimpse the Impossible to dig for combo pieces and ensure a smooth execution of its game plan. The land base is specifically tailored to support the combo strategy.

The deck revolves around utilizing various Sliver creatures with a focus on boosting their power and providing evasion. By combining abilities like buffing each other, generating mana, and giving deathtouch or flying, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of powerful and hard-to-block creatures. The inclusion of cards like Mutagenic Growth and Winding Way helps to further enhance the deck's aggression and consistency in order to secure a fast victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing a combination of counter spells, removal spells, and card draw to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It also includes some creatures that can generate tokens or provide additional value. The mana base includes snow lands and cards that benefit from having snow permanents. The goal is to outlast the opponent by countering key spells, removing threats, and eventually overwhelming them with efficient creatures and burn spells.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through card advantage and disruption while ramping to assemble the Urza Tron lands for big mana plays. Utilizing a combination of card draw, recursion, and counter magic, the deck aims to stall the game until it can overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells and finishers like Rolling Thunder. The key strategy is to efficiently manage resources, protect key pieces, and out-value the opponent in the late game.

The key concept of this deck is to utilize walls as a defensive strategy while assembling a combo with cards like Freed from the Real and Axebane Guardian to generate large amounts of mana. This deck aims to quickly ramp up to play powerful spells or creatures, such as Valakut Invoker, and overwhelm the opponent with a strong board presence. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help to refill the hand with creatures, while Reaping the Graves provides graveyard recursion for key combo pieces. Overall, this deck focuses on creating a solid defense and explosive combo potential to secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly swarming the board with low-cost creatures and utilizing various combat tricks to overwhelm the opponent. The key strategy is to maintain a steady stream of aggressive creatures while also being able to protect them with spells like Prismatic Strands. By combining creatures with efficient combat abilities and spell support, the deck aims to exert pressure on the opponent early and maintain a strong board presence throughout the game. The deck includes a mix of flying and ground creatures to present different threats and keep the opponent on their toes.
